name: python3-click-didyoumean
title: python3-click-didyoumean
summary: enables git-like did-you-mean feature in click
description:
enables git-like did-you-mean feature in click
Click is a Python package for creating beautiful command line interfaces
in a composable way with as little code as necessary.  It's the "Command
Line Interface Creation Kit".  It's highly configurable but comes with
sensible defaults out of the box.
.
It aims to make the process of writing command line tools quick and fun
while also preventing any frustration caused by the inability to implement
an intended CLI API.
.
This package add the feature to do git-like did-you-mean feature in click.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use python3-click-didyoumean because enables git-like did-you-mean feature in click.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use python3-click-didyoumean as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair python3-click-didyoumean with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `python3-click-didyoumean --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
